content: State-of-the-art 2024-2025 curiosity-driven active memory for agents can be repurposed to dynamically prune and prioritize the exponentially large joint search space of Task and Motion Planning (TAMP) by encoding prior successful task-motion trajectories as curiosity-weighted memory retrieval cues. This creates a hybrid system where unexpected, high-information new motion solutions are actively sampled for long-term retention instead of being discarded after a single planning run, continuously shrinking the search space for future planning problems.
